jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation followed by a scream
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic 'D'oh!' - perfectly integrated character catchphrase that became culturally significant

jokes.text: You may remember me from such films as The Erotic Adventures of Hercules and Dial 'M' for Murderousness
jokes.setup: Troy's standard introduction formula
jokes.punchline: Absurd fake movie titles that parody real films
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy McClure formula with clever wordplay on actual films - high character integration

jokes.text: Excitement, she wrote
jokes.setup: Angela Lansbury walking on hot coals
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Murder, She Wrote' with pain reaction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that transforms the TV show title into commentary on physical pain

jokes.text: [Gasps] Moe, don't throw out that brine
jokes.setup: Watching someone walk on hot coals
jokes.punchline: Barney's alcoholic concern for pickle brine over human suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney character moment - his alcoholism makes him prioritize bar supplies over human concern

jokes.text: I'll be played by Jimmy Smits
jokes.setup: Krusty's battle with addiction becoming a TV movie
jokes.punchline: Unlikely casting choice for the clown
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdity in casting - decent but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Not the face!
jokes.setup: Krusty facing three Siberian tigers
jokes.punchline: His vain concern about his appearance while being mauled
jokes.explanation: Classic vanity joke during physical peril - works for Krusty's character but fairly standard

jokes.text: But, Marge, Alan Thicke is throwing knives at Ricardo Montalban
jokes.setup: Marge wants Homer to come home due to snow
jokes.punchline: Homer's absurd justification involving random celebrities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - prioritizing bizarre TV over practical concerns with specific celebrity absurdity

jokes.text: Take it easy, folks. It's 'snow' picnic out there. / I 'snow' what you mean. / You're dead weight, Marty
jokes.setup: Radio DJs making weather puns
jokes.punchline: Escalating terrible puns followed by brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from bad puns to workplace hostility - satirizes morning radio dynamics

jokes.text: D'oh! Well, I got him as good as he got me
jokes.setup: Homer crashes into another car
jokes.punchline: His delusional assessment of mutual damage
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic self-delusion after obvious failure - good character consistency

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Another crash or mishap
jokes.punchline: The catchphrase again
jokes.explanation: Repetitive use of the catchphrase - less impactful than first instance

jokes.text: Don't tell him you were at a bar. [Gasps] But what else is open at night? It's a pornography store. I was buying pornography
jokes.setup: Homer needs to explain where he was during the accident
jokes.punchline: His logic leads him to an even more embarrassing admission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Homer's flawed logic makes him choose a worse lie. Perfect character writing with excellent structure

jokes.text: [Thinking] Heh-heh-heh. I would've never thought of that
jokes.setup: Homer's pornography store explanation
jokes.punchline: Agent's impressed reaction to the elaborate lie
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Homer's lie - the agent's admiration makes it even more absurd

jokes.text: Can't trust a pig with watermelons, you know
jokes.setup: Explaining why Zeke rides up front
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical folk wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd non-logic delivered with complete confidence - great character moment

jokes.text: She'll go 300 hectares on a single tank of kerosene
jokes.setup: Describing the car's efficiency
jokes.punchline: Mixing measurement systems and wrong fuel type
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mix of measurement confusion and completely wrong fuel - became a quotable classic

jokes.text: What country is this car from? It no longer exists
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the car's origin
jokes.punchline: Implying the country was destroyed or dissolved
jokes.explanation: Dark geopolitical humor delivered casually - brilliant economy and became highly quotable

jokes.text: Put it in 'H'
jokes.setup: Giving driving instructions
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical gear designation
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdity - 'H' makes no sense as a gear. Maximum economy with huge quotability

jokes.text: Do you come with the car? Oh, you. [Giggles] - Do you come with the car? Oh, you. [Giggles]
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the promotional woman
jokes.punchline: Repeating the exact same exchange twice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy through repetition - Homer's obliviousness makes the repeat awkward

jokes.text: Bart! That car belonged to Bonnie and Clyde. Show a little respect
jokes.setup: Bart making gun noises at a car
jokes.punchline: Demanding respect for notorious criminals
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - asking for respect for criminals while scolding innocent play

jokes.text: Hey, wait, That's not a dummy. This exhibit is closed
jokes.setup: Crash test demonstration
jokes.punchline: Implying they used a real person in the crash test
jokes.explanation: Dark humor suggesting automotive testing gone wrong - good shock value

jokes.text: Dad, that's not the real Batman. Of course I'm Batman. - See, here's a picture of me with Robin. - Who the hell's Robin?
jokes.setup: Adam West insisting he's Batman
jokes.punchline: Bart's generational ignorance of Robin
jokes.explanation: Good generational humor - Adam West's delusion meets modern kid's ignorance

jokes.text: Michelle Pfeiffer. Ha. The only true Catwoman is Julie Newmar, Lee Meriwether or Eartha Kitt
jokes.setup: Discussion of Batman movies
jokes.punchline: West's passionate defense of classic Catwomen
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Adam West - his passionate commitment to Batman history

jokes.text: And I didn't need molded plastic to improve my physique. Pure West
jokes.setup: Comparing himself to modern Batman
jokes.punchline: His delusional pride in his own physique
jokes.explanation: Excellent character delusion - West's pride in his clearly inferior physique

jokes.text: And how come Batman doesn't dance anymore? Remember the Batusi?
jokes.setup: West lamenting modern Batman
jokes.punchline: Reference to the ridiculous 60s Batman dance
jokes.explanation: Perfect nostalgia for the campy 60s Batman - West's character completely committed

jokes.text: Nice meetin' ya. Just keep moving. Don't make eye contact
jokes.setup: Adam West's bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: Security treating him like a dangerous mental patient
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to West's oddness - treating celebrity delusion as mental illness

jokes.text: Homer, I've got to sneak these valuable artworks out of the White House but I can't get out of my driveway because of these protesters. Mr. President, those young people are about to get a dose of reality
jokes.setup: Homer fantasizing about the plow's capabilities
jokes.punchline: Imagining the President needs his help with protestors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's grandiose fantasies to presidential level

jokes.text: Your wife? [Imitates Whipcrack] What, you think I'm gonna buy a $20,000 truck just because you make that noise? [Imitates Whipcracks] I'll take it!
jokes.setup: Salesman mocking Homer for consulting his wife
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately caves to the whip-crack sound effects
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff structure - Homer's complete lack of willpower perfectly executed

jokes.text: If you're gonna get mad at me every time I do something stupid then I guess I'll just have to stop doing stupid things. - Good. - Fine. I'll never ever do another stupid thing
jokes.setup: Homer defending his plow purchase
jokes.punchline: His impossible promise to stop being himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - promising to fundamentally change his nature rather than admit fault

jokes.text: [Walking into door] Oh, Homie. Didn't that hurt? [Weakly] No
jokes.setup: Homer's promise to not do stupid things
jokes.punchline: Immediately walking into door and denying it hurt
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate payoff to previous joke - Homer's stubbornness even about obvious pain

jokes.text: Twenty percent off at Lullabuys. Just tell 'em Big Baby sent you. I know you can hear me. I'm talkin' to you. You sicken me
jokes.setup: Character handing out flyers being ignored
jokes.punchline: Escalating anger at being ignored ending in hostility
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from friendly promotion to hostile confrontation - realistic street character

jokes.text: Well, at least I can't sink any lower. Come back, diaper. Come back
jokes.setup: Big Baby saying he can't sink lower
jokes.punchline: Immediately chasing after a blown-away diaper
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - immediately proving himself wrong with pathetic action

jokes.text: Dear Lord, in your infinite wisdom you know the number to call when you need a plow is Klondike 5-3226
jokes.setup: Homer reading from the Bible at church
jokes.punchline: Turning religious reading into commercial advertisement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sacrilege - Homer's shameless self-promotion in sacred context is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Homer, this is really low. Not as low as my low, low prices
jokes.setup: Marge calling out Homer's inappropriate behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer turning moral criticism into sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - unable to break character even when being morally criticized

jokes.text: Dad, who's watching TV at 3:17 a.m.? Alcoholics, the unemployable, angry loners
jokes.setup: Question about late-night TV audience
jokes.punchline: Homer's brutal but accurate demographic assessment
jokes.explanation: Sharp social observation delivered matter-of-factly - dark truth about late-night demographics

jokes.text: [Together] Old Man Winter! [Cackling] That's right. I fill your driveways with ice and snow. What are you gonna do about it? Nothin'. That's what
jokes.setup: Kids recognizing a character on TV
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphized winter being deliberately antagonistic
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist concept - winter as a malicious character taunting people

jokes.text: Are you tired of having your hands cut off by snowblowers and the inevitable heart attacks that come with shoveling snow?
jokes.setup: Standard infomercial opening
jokes.punchline: Extreme escalation to dismemberment and death
jokes.explanation: Good infomercial parody - escalating normal problems to deadly consequences

jokes.text: You are fully bonded and licensed by the city, aren't you, Mr. Plow? Shut up, boy
jokes.setup: Bart asking legitimate business question during commercial
jokes.punchline: Homer's unprofessional response on air
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's inability to maintain professionalism even in his own commercial

jokes.text: Well, John Q. Driveway has our number. Now we play the waiting game. Ah, the waiting game sucks. Let's play Hungry Hungry Hippos
jokes.setup: Homer philosophically accepting the wait
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning patience for children's game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer attention span - from wise businessman to impatient child in seconds

jokes.text: I'm cold and frightened
jokes.setup: People celebrating being freed by Homer's plowing
jokes.punchline: One person's honest vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to celebration - one person's honest fear amid others' joy

jokes.text: Say your prayers, plow boy!
jokes.setup: Homer has cleared the roads for school
jokes.punchline: Bus driver's murderous anger at having to work
jokes.explanation: Good ironic reversal - Homer's helpful act creates an enemy rather than gratitude

jokes.text: You didn't even give a beer to those freed Iranian hostages. Ah, they shouldn't have been there in the first place
jokes.setup: Homer getting free beer while hostages didn't
jokes.punchline: Moe's victim-blaming attitude
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor - Moe's twisted logic about international hostages vs. local snow removal

jokes.text: Well, wishing won't make it so. You gotta pull up your diaper, get out there and be the best damn Barney you can be
jokes.setup: Barney wishing he was a hero
jokes.punchline: Moe's mangled motivational speech with baby reference
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment - mixing tough guy advice with infantilizing language

jokes.text: This might sound silly, but just for a change, would you mind-- Cutting my nails? Brushing my teeth? No, no. Could you wear the Mr. Plow jacket? Just, uh, for fun
jokes.setup: Marge asking for something that might sound silly
jokes.punchline: Innocent request for costume play instead of expected intimate acts
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - suggesting something sexual but revealing innocent costume fetish

jokes.text: Say hello to the Plow King. Barney, you stole my idea
jokes.setup: Barney appearing with competing plow business
jokes.punchline: Homer's accusation of idea theft for obvious concept
jokes.explanation: Standard setup for competition storyline - Homer's possessiveness over obvious business idea

jokes.text: Linda Ronstadt? How'd you get her? Ah, we've been looking for a project to do together for a while
jokes.setup: Linda Ronstadt appearing in Barney's commercial
jokes.punchline: Barney's casual claim of long-term collaboration with major star
jokes.explanation: Good absurd celebrity connection - Barney's delusion about having ongoing relationship with major star

jokes.text: I've got two words for you. Mellow out, man
jokes.setup: Homer giving advice to study-focused Barney
jokes.punchline: Counting 'mellow out, man' as two words
jokes.explanation: Standard 'bad at counting' joke - not particularly original but fits Homer's character

jokes.text: Hey! Where have you been all my life?
jokes.setup: Barney trying his first beer
jokes.punchline: Immediate addiction/love declaration
jokes.explanation: Important character origin moment - explains Barney's transformation from scholar to drunk

jokes.text: I'll take your money, but I'm not gonna plow your driveway
jokes.setup: Homer angry at Flanders for switching services
jokes.punchline: Illogical business decision out of spite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - prioritizing emotional satisfaction over business sense

jokes.text: So long, Superman. Your secret identity is safe with me
jokes.setup: Homer leaving after failed job interview
jokes.punchline: Adam West confusing Homer with another superhero
jokes.explanation: Good callback to West's character - his superhero delusions extend to identifying others as heroes

jokes.text: Yeah. They were gay
jokes.setup: Mention of Leave It to Beaver
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual revisionist interpretation
jokes.explanation: Edgy for the time but not particularly clever - relies on shock value rather than wit

jokes.text: I know. I'll do a rap. [Imitating Beat Box] [Rapping about Mr. Plow]
jokes.setup: Homer needing fresh advertising approach
jokes.punchline: His embarrassing attempt at rap music
jokes.explanation: Standard 'white guy can't rap' joke - culturally relevant for the era but not innovative

jokes.text: You know those radio ads where two people with annoying voices yammer back and forth? I invented those. Ow! Happens all the time
jokes.setup: Ad executive describing annoying radio format
jokes.punchline: Taking credit for universally hated advertising style plus immediate injury
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on advertising plus casual acceptance of recurring injury

jokes.text: These look like teeth marks. I thought there was chocolate inside. It was never wrapped in foil. Well, why was it wrapped in foil? It was never wrapped in foil
jokes.setup: Examining damaged key to the city
jokes.punchline: Aide's nonsensical explanation for why Homer bit it
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic loop - the aide's circular reasoning about foil wrapping

jokes.text: There's a $10,000 bill in it for you. Oh, yeah? Which president's on it? Uh, all of them. They're having a party. Jimmy Carter's passed out on the couch
jokes.setup: Homer trying to lure Barney with fake money
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy about presidential party on currency
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating lie - Homer's improvisation creates vivid absurd imagery that became iconic

jokes.text: Kiss my asphalt. So I says, 'Kiss my asphalt.' Hmm? Hmm?
jokes.setup: Customer asking Homer not to scrape the driveway
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud pun and expectation of appreciation
jokes.explanation: Decent pun with perfect Homer follow-up - his pride in mediocre wordplay is very in-character

jokes.text: Hey, this old girl's as sure-footed as a mountain goat. [Goat bleating and falling sounds]
jokes.setup: Homer boasting about his truck's stability
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by actual mountain goat falling off cliff
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual gag timing. Homer's confidence immediately undercut by reality contradicting his metaphor.

jokes.text: I'll be reunited with my loved ones-- my dad and that plant I never watered
jokes.setup: Homer facing death and thinking of reunion
jokes.punchline: Equating dead father with neglected plant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Homer's emotional equivalence between human father and houseplant is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: When two best friends work together not even God himself can stop them. [Booming Male Voice] Oh, no?
jokes.setup: Homer's confident declaration about friendship
jokes.punchline: Immediate divine contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure - Homer's hubris immediately answered by the ultimate authority. Brilliant timing and economy

jokes.text: Well, if 70° days in the middle of winter are the price of car pollution you'll forgive me if I keep my old Pontiac
jokes.setup: News report about unusual weather
jokes.punchline: Anchor's selfish embrace of environmental destruction
jokes.explanation: Sharp environmental satire - journalist prioritizing personal comfort over planetary health

jokes.text: [Man] Hello. This is the Repo Depot. I'm just calling to distract you while we repossess your plow. Oh, yeah? How dumb do you think I-- Oh
jokes.setup: Repo man's honest distraction tactic
jokes.punchline: Homer falling for it despite being warned
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup - the honesty about deception makes it even funnier when it works perfectly

jokes.text: Our forecast calls for flurries of passion followed by extended periods of gettin' it on
jokes.setup: Homer doing weather-style romantic forecast
jokes.punchline: Mixing meteorology with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent romantic humor - Homer's attempt at sexy talk using his recent weather experience
